name: gitanalyzer

metadata.name: GitAnalyzer
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: A toolkit for Mining Software Repositories (MSR)

documentation: GitAnalyzer is a versatile Python toolkit for analyzing Git repositories. It simplifies the process of extracting data such as commits, contributors, file changes, diffs, and source code, and supports exporting data to CSV format.

modules.name: gitanalyzer.repository
documentation: Core module containing the GitRepo class, which serves as the primary interface for GitAnalyzer.

classes.name: gitanalyzer.repository.GitRepo

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.GitRepo.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a GitRepo instance for analysis.

Required parameter:
- repository_path: Path to local repository or list of paths. 
  Supports both local paths and remote URLs.

Optional parameters control various aspects of the analysis:
- Commit selection (dates, hashes, tags)
- Analysis behavior (threading, file types, etc.)
- Git options (whitespace handling, mailmap usage, etc.)

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.GitRepo._is_remote_repo
documentation: Check if the given path is a remote repository URL.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.GitRepo._get_clone_directory
documentation: Determine the directory for cloning repositories.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.GitRepo._clone_remote_repository
documentation: Clone a remote repository to the specified directory.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.GitRepo._prepare_repository
documentation: Prepare repository for analysis and cleanup afterward.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.GitRepo._extract_repo_name
documentation: Extract repository name from URL.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.GitRepo.analyze_commits
documentation:
Analyze repository commits based on configured filters.
Returns a generator yielding Commit objects.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.GitRepo._process_commit
documentation: Process individual commits and apply filters.
Primary class of GitAnalyzer that manages repository analysis operations.

classes.name: gitanalyzer.repository.InvalidRepositoryURL

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.InvalidRepositoryURL.analyze_repository_commits
documentation:
Process and analyze all commits in the repository based on configured filters.
Yields commit objects that match the specified criteria.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.InvalidRepositoryURL._process_commits_parallel
documentation: Handle parallel processing of commits using thread pool.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.InvalidRepositoryURL._analyze_single_commit
documentation: Analyze an individual commit and determine if it should be included.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.InvalidRepositoryURL._process_file_specific_commits
documentation: Process commits specific to target file.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.InvalidRepositoryURL._process_tagged_commits
documentation: Process commits that are tagged.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.InvalidRepositoryURL._setup_repository_context
documentation: Set up repository context and handle cleanup.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.InvalidRepositoryURL._get_repository_path
documentation: Get local repository path, cloning if necessary.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.InvalidRepositoryURL._cleanup_repository
documentation: Clean up repository resources.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.InvalidRepositoryURL._split_in_chunks
documentation:
Divide commits into balanced chunks for parallel processing.

Args:
    commits: Complete list of commits to process
    worker_count: Number of parallel workers to distribute work across
    
Returns:
    List of commit chunks for parallel processing

methods.name: gitanalyzer.repository.InvalidRepositoryURL._extract_repo_name
documentation:
Extract repository name from a git URL.

Args:
    url: Git repository URL to parse
    
Returns:
    Repository name extracted from URL
    
Raises:
    InvalidRepositoryURL: If URL format is invalid
Raised when a repository URL is malformed.

classes.name: gitanalyzer.repository.InvalidRepositoryURL
documentation: Raised when a repository URL is malformed or invalid.

modules.name: gitanalyzer.domain.developer
documentation:
Module containing the Developer entity for representing Git contributors.
Repository: https://github.com/codingwithshawnyt/GitAnalyzer

classes.name: gitanalyzer.domain.developer.Developer

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.developer.Developer.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a new Developer instance.

Args:
    full_name: The developer's full name (optional)
    contact_email: The developer's email address (optional)

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.developer.Developer.__eq__
documentation:
Compare this developer with another object for equality.

Args:
    other: Object to compare with
    
Returns:
    bool: True if both objects are equal, False otherwise

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.developer.Developer.__hash__
documentation:
Generate a hash value for the Developer instance.

Returns:
    int: Hash value based on the string representation

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.developer.Developer.to_string
documentation:
Create a string representation of the developer.

Returns:
    str: Formatted string with name and email

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.developer.Developer.__str__
documentation:
String representation for informal display.

Returns:
    str: Human-readable string representation

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.developer.Developer.__repr__
documentation:
String representation for debugging and development.

Returns:
    str: Detailed string representation for reproduction

Represents a Git contributor with their identifying information.
Stores basic details like name and contact information.

modules.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit
documentation:
Classes and utilities for handling commit-related operations, including
CommitChanges, ChangeType, and CodeMethod.

classes.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.ChangeType
documentation:
Represents different types of changes that can occur in a commit.
Available types: ADDITION, DUPLICATE, MOVED, REMOVED, CHANGED, UNDEFINED

classes.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.MaintenanceMetric
documentation: Defines the key metrics used in the Delta Maintenance Model for code analysis.

classes.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CodeMethod

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CodeMethod.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a CodeMethod instance using Lizard's analysis results.
Extracts and stores various metrics about the method.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CodeMethod.__eq__
documentation: Compare two methods for equality based on name and parameters.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CodeMethod.__hash__
documentation: Generate a unique hash for the method based on name and parameters.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CodeMethod.check_risk_level
documentation:
Determine if the method is low-risk according to the specified metric.

Args:
    metric: The maintenance metric to evaluate

Returns:
    bool: True if the method is considered low-risk for the given metric

Represents a code method/function with its associated metrics and properties.
Analyzes and stores various code quality metrics using Lizard.

classes.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new FileChange instance to track modifications to a file.

Args:
    diff_object: Git diff object containing change information

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.__hash__
documentation:
Generates a unique hash for the file change using SHA-256,
based on the change type, path, and content.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange._determine_change_type
documentation: Determines the type of change made to the file.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.diff_text
documentation: Returns the diff text in UTF-8 format.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange._decode_text
documentation: Safely decodes content to UTF-8 string format.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.content
documentation: Returns the current file content in raw bytes.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.previous_content
documentation: Returns the previous version's content in raw bytes.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange._get_raw_content
documentation: Extracts raw content from a git blob object.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.current_code
documentation: Returns the current version's source code as text.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.previous_code
documentation: Returns the previous version's source code as text.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.lines_added
documentation: Counts the number of lines added in this change.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.lines_removed
documentation: Counts the number of lines removed in this change.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.original_path
documentation:
Gets the file's original path before changes.
Returns None for newly added files.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.current_path
documentation:
Gets the file's current path after changes.
Returns None for deleted files.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.filename
documentation:
Extracts the base filename from the path.
Uses current path if available, falls back to original path.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.is_analyzable
documentation:
Checks if the file's language is supported for analysis.
Uses Lizard's language detection based on file extension.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.lines_of_code
documentation:
Returns the total lines of code in the file.
Triggers metric calculation if not already done.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.cyclomatic_complexity
documentation:
Returns the file's cyclomatic complexity score.
Triggers metric calculation if not already done.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.token_count
documentation:
Returns the total count of tokens in the file.
Triggers metric calculation if not already done.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.parsed_diff
documentation:
Parses the diff into a structured format showing added and deleted lines.

Returns:
    Dictionary with keys 'added' and 'deleted', each containing a list of
    tuples (line_number, line_content)

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange._parse_chunk_header
documentation:
Extracts line numbers from a git diff chunk header.

Args:
    line: Diff chunk header line starting with @@
    
Returns:
    Tuple of (deletion_start, addition_start) line numbers

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.current_methods
documentation:
Analyzes and returns all methods in the current version of the file.
Includes metrics like complexity, size, and parameters.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.previous_methods
documentation:
Analyzes and returns all methods from the previous version of the file.
Includes complete method information before changes.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.modified_methods
documentation:
Identifies and returns methods that were modified in this change.
Compares both versions of the file to detect changes accurately.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange._calculate_risk_profile
documentation:
Calculates the risk distribution for a set of methods.

Args:
    methods: List of methods to analyze
    metric: Maintenance metric to evaluate risk
    
Returns:
    Tuple of (low_risk_volume, high_risk_volume) in lines of code

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange._compute_risk_delta
documentation:
Calculates how the risk profile changed between versions.

Args:
    metric: Maintenance metric to evaluate
    
Returns:
    Tuple of (low_risk_change, high_risk_change) in lines of code

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange._compute_code_metrics
documentation:
Analyzes code metrics using Lizard for current and optionally previous versions.

Args:
    analyze_previous: Whether to analyze the previous version of the code

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange._decode_content
documentation:
Attempts to decode binary content to UTF-8 string.

Args:
    content: Binary content to decode
    
Returns:
    Decoded string or None if decoding fails

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.FileChange.__eq__
documentation: Checks equality between two FileChange objects.

Represents a file that has been modified within a commit, tracking its
changes and metrics.

classes.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new CommitInfo instance.

Args:
    git_commit: GitPython commit object
    config: Analysis configuration settings

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.__hash__
documentation:
Generates a hash based on the commit's SHA.
Uses the commit's hexadecimal SHA to ensure uniqueness.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.sha
documentation: Returns the commit's SHA identifier.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.author
documentation: Returns the primary author of the commit.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.collaborators
documentation: Returns all additional contributors to the commit.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.code_reviewer
documentation: Returns the developer who reviewed and committed the changes.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.repository_name
documentation: Returns the name of the Git repository.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.repository_path
documentation: Returns the full filesystem path to the repository.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.creation_date
documentation: Returns when the changes were originally authored.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.commit_date
documentation: Returns when the changes were committed to the repository.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.author_timezone_offset
documentation: Returns the author's timezone offset in seconds from UTC.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.commit_timezone_offset
documentation: Returns the committer's timezone offset in seconds from UTC.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.message
documentation: Returns the commit's description message.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.parent_commits
documentation: Returns list of parent commit SHAs.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.is_merge
documentation: Indicates if this is a merge commit.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo._compute_stats
documentation: Calculates commit statistics if not already cached.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo._parse_git_stats
documentation:
Parses git diff statistics into a structured format.

Args:
    stats_text: Raw git diff stats output
    
Returns:
    Dictionary containing parsed statistics

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.added_lines
documentation: Returns the total number of lines added in this commit.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.removed_lines
documentation: Returns the total number of lines removed in this commit.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.total_changes
documentation: Returns the total number of line changes (additions + deletions).

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.changed_files
documentation: Returns the number of files modified in this commit.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.file_changes
documentation:
Returns a list of all file changes in this commit.
Note: For merge commits, an empty list is returned due to complexity
of parsing merge conflicts. See:
- https://haacked.com/archive/2014/02/21/reviewing-merge-commits/
- https://github.com/ishepard/pydriller/issues/89#issuecomment-590243707

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo._process_diff_data
documentation:
Processes raw git diff data into FileChange objects.

Args:
    diff_data: List of git diff objects
    
Returns:
    List of FileChange objects representing the modifications

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.is_in_main_branch
documentation: Indicates whether this commit is part of the main branch.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.containing_branches
documentation: Returns all branches that contain this commit.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.maintainability_size_metric
documentation:
Calculates the maintainability metric for method sizes.

Returns a value between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating the proportion of positive
maintainability changes. Higher values (closer to 1.0) indicate improvements
like splitting large methods or working on small methods. Lower values
indicate work on methods that remain or become too large.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.maintainability_complexity_metric
documentation:
Calculates the maintainability metric for method complexity.

Returns a value between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating the proportion of positive
maintainability changes. Higher values indicate improvements like reducing
method complexity, while lower values indicate work on methods that remain
or become more complex.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.maintainability_interface_metric
documentation:
Calculates the maintainability metric for method interfaces.

Returns a value between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating the proportion of positive
maintainability changes. Higher values indicate improvements like reducing
parameter counts, while lower values indicate work on methods that maintain
or increase large parameter lists.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo._calculate_maintainability_score
documentation:
Computes a maintainability score based on the given metric.

Evaluates changes as either positive (adding low-risk or removing high-risk code)
or negative (adding high-risk or removing low-risk code).

Args:
    metric: The maintenance metric to evaluate
    
Returns:
    Float between 0.0 and 1.0 representing the proportion of positive changes,
    or None if no supported files were modified

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo._calculate_risk_changes
documentation:
Analyzes how the risk profile changed in this commit.

Args:
    metric: The maintenance metric to evaluate
    
Returns:
    Tuple of (low_risk_change, high_risk_change) in lines of code,
    or None if no supported files were modified

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo._calculate_improvement_ratio
documentation:
Calculates the ratio of positive maintenance changes to total changes.

Args:
    low_risk_change: Net change in low-risk code
    high_risk_change: Net change in high-risk code
    
Returns:
    Ratio of positive changes (0.0 to 1.0), or None if no changes

methods.name: gitanalyzer.domain.commit.CommitInfo.__eq__
documentation: Checks if two CommitInfo objects are equal.

Represents a Git commit with its associated metadata and changes.
Provides access to commit details like hash, author, dates, and modified files.

modules.name: gitanalyzer.git
documentation:
GitAnalyzer's core Git repository interface module.
This module contains the GitRepo class for Git repository analysis.

classes.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a new Git repository interface.

Args:
    repository_path (str): Path to the Git repository
    config (Configuration, optional): Custom configuration settings

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.repository
documentation:
Access the underlying GitPython repository object.

Returns:
    Repo: The GitPython repository instance

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.cleanup
documentation:
Clean up repository resources.
Important for Windows systems where GitPython may leak resources.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo._initialize_repository
documentation: Set up the Git repository connection and configure basic settings.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo._detect_primary_branch
documentation: Identify and store the repository's primary branch name.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.get_current_commit
documentation:
Retrieve the current HEAD commit.

Returns:
    Commit: The current HEAD commit

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.get_commits
documentation:
Generate a sequence of repository commits.

Args:
    revision (str): Starting revision/commit (defaults to HEAD)
    **kwargs: Additional arguments for commit iteration

Yields:
    Generator[Commit]: A sequence of commit objects

Raises:
    Exception: If there's an error accessing commits

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo._convert_git_commit
documentation:
Convert a GitPython commit to a GitAnalyzer commit object.

Args:
    git_commit (GitPythonCommit): GitPython commit object

Returns:
    Commit: GitAnalyzer commit object

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.get_commit_by_hash
documentation:
Retrieve a specific commit by its hash.

Args:
    commit_hash (str): Hash identifier of the commit

Returns:
    Commit: The requested commit object

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.convert_gitpython_commit
documentation:
Convert a native GitPython commit object to a GitAnalyzer commit.
This is an internal method primarily used for system integration.

Args:
    git_commit (GitPythonCommit): Native GitPython commit object

Returns:
    Commit: Converted GitAnalyzer commit object

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.switch_to_commit
documentation:
Switch the repository state to a specific commit.

Warning:
    This operation modifies the repository state and is not thread-safe.
    Use with caution in multi-threaded environments.

Args:
    commit_hash (str): Hash of the target commit

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.list_repository_files
documentation:
Get all files in the repository, excluding the .git directory.

Returns:
    List[str]: Full paths of all files in the repository

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.reset_to_main
documentation:
Reset the repository to the main branch, discarding all local changes.
Uses force checkout to ensure clean state.

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.count_total_commits
documentation:
Get the total number of commits in the repository.

Returns:
    int: Total number of commits

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.get_commit_by_tag
documentation:
Retrieve a commit referenced by a specific tag.

Args:
    tag_name (str): Name of the tag

Returns:
    Commit: The commit referenced by the tag

Raises:
    IndexError, AttributeError: If tag doesn't exist or is invalid

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.get_all_tagged_commits
documentation:
Get commit hashes for all tagged commits in the repository.

Returns:
    List[str]: List of commit hashes that have tags

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.analyze_commit_changes
documentation:
Analyze which commits last modified the lines changed in a given commit.
Implements SZZ algorithm to track line modifications.

Process:
1. Extract file differences
2. Identify removed lines
3. Use git blame to find commits that introduced the modified lines

Args:
    commit (Commit): Target commit to analyze
    specific_file (ChangedFile, optional): Limit analysis to specific file
    ignore_commits_file (str, optional): Path to file listing commits to ignore

Returns:
    Dict[str, Set[str]]: Mapping of files to sets of commit hashes

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.compare_commits
documentation:
Compare two commits and get the list of modified files between them.

Args:
    start_commit (str): Hash of the starting commit
    end_commit (str): Hash of the ending commit

Returns:
    List[ChangedFile]: List of files modified between the commits

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo._analyze_commit_history
documentation:
Analyze the commit history to find the origin of changed lines.

Args:
    commit (Commit): The commit to analyze
    changes (List[ChangedFile]): List of file changes to analyze
    ignore_hashes_file (Optional[str]): Path to file containing commit hashes to ignore

Returns:
    Dict[str, Set[str]]: Mapping of file paths to sets of related commit hashes

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo._get_file_blame
documentation:
Get git blame information for a file at a specific commit.

Args:
    commit_hash (str): Hash of the commit
    file_path (str): Path to the file
    ignore_hashes_file (Optional[str]): Path to file containing hashes to ignore

Returns:
    List[str]: Lines of git blame output

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo._is_significant_line
documentation:
Determine if a line contains significant code (not comments or whitespace).

Args:
    line (str): The line to check

Returns:
    bool: True if the line contains significant code, False otherwise

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.get_file_commit_history
documentation:
Get the complete commit history for a specific file.

Args:
    file_path (str): Path to the file
    include_deletions (bool): Whether to include commits where the file was deleted

Returns:
    List[str]: List of commit hashes that modified the file

methods.name: gitanalyzer.git.GitRepo.__del__
documentation: Cleanup repository resources when the object is destroyed.

Core class for Git repository analysis in GitAnalyzer.
Provides functionality for commit analysis, checkout operations, and repository management.
